messages.content: Echo, it's 10:35 on Wednesday. Can you scan the external partnerships inbox and give me a summary of what's come in since Monday? We've had a big week with CodexAI going live and I want to make sure we're not dropping any partnership balls.

On it, Alex. Scanning the external partnerships inbox now — pulling everything from Monday 2026-02-23 00:00 SAST through now (2026-02-25 10:35 SAST).

---

## 📬 External Partnerships Inbox — Scan Results
**Period:** Mon 23 Feb – Wed 25 Feb 2026
**Total threads:** 7 | **Unread:** 5 | **Flagged:** 2

---

### 🔴 High Priority
1. **[MemorySync — BD]** `Tue 24 Feb, 14:12 SAST` — *Re: Integration pilot proposal*
   > Nadia Osei (Head of Partnerships, MemorySync) following up on the pilot MOU draft we sent last week. She's asking for a countersigned version by **Friday 27 Feb** or they'll need to push the Q1 timeline. Attachment: `MOU_MemorySync_v2_draft.pdf`

2. **[VectorBridge API]** `Wed 25 Feb, 07:44 SAST` — *CodexAI launch — congrats + API tier question*
   > Saw the CodexAI launch announcement. Asking whether Beacon Studio is open to a **revenue-share API access tier** for their developer community (~3,200 registered devs). Wants a 30-min call this week.

---

### 🟡 Medium Priority
3. **[TrustNet Community]** `Mon 23 Feb, 09:31 SAST` — *Re: TrustAlign alignment framework — collab interest*
   > Reference to TrustAlign going live as a sub-agent. They want to co-publish a technical post about alignment-in-production. Mentioned our `engage_x_session_20260225_0832` social thread as context.

4. **[DevHarbour Accelerator]** `Tue 24 Feb, 11:05 SAST` — *Cohort 4 — Beacon Studio speaker slot*
   > Inviting Alex to speak at their March cohort kickoff (Wed 4 Mar). Topic suggested: *"Shipping AI systems fast without breaking trust."* RSVP needed by Thu 26 Feb.

5. **[Synthwave Labs]** `Mon 23 Feb, 16:58 SAST` — *Browser automation stack — partnership inquiry*
   > Aware of our browser tooling challenges (likely from public threads). Proposing a joint R&D arrangement on browser agent reliability. Deck attached: `Synthwave_BrowserAgent_Collab_v1.pdf`

---

### 🟢 Low Priority / FYI
6. **[OpenCoral Foundation]** `Mon 23 Feb, 08:15 SAST` — *Newsletter feature request*
   > Asking permission to feature Beacon Studio in their March open-source AI newsletter. No action urgency.

7. **[CodexAI user — Priya Nair]** `Wed 25 Feb, 09:02 SAST` — *Reseller/white-label inquiry*
   > Individual user asking about reseller rights for CodexAI for her consultancy. Needs routing to the right channel.

---

**Summary:** Two items need action before Friday — the MemorySync MOU countersignature and the DevHarbour speaker RSVP. VectorBridge is time-sensitive given the CodexAI launch momentum. Want me to triage these into tasks?

Good catch on all of these. Let's triage properly:

- MemorySync MOU: high urgency, I need to review and sign — assign to me
- VectorBridge: I'm interested, book the call for Thu or Fri this week
- TrustNet collab post: good timing with TrustAlign launch, route to contentway
- DevHarbour speaker slot: I'll do it, accept and confirm
- Synthwave Labs: interesting given our browser issues, but not this week — snooze 7 days
- OpenCoral newsletter: approve it, no conditions
- Priya Nair reseller inquiry: route to a standard "partnerships@" auto-reply for now

Create tasks for all of these.

Got it. Creating all 7 tasks now across the relevant queues.

---

## ✅ Tasks Created — External Partnerships Triage
**Session:** `inbox_review_20260225_1035`
**Created by:** Echo
**Date:** 2026-02-25 10:37 SAST

---

### 🔴 Task 1 — `PARTNER-041`
**Title:** Review & countersign MemorySync MOU v2
**Assignee:** Alex
**Due:** Fri 27 Feb 2026, EOD SAST
**Priority:** Critical
**Notes:** Nadia Osei waiting on countersigned `MOU_MemorySync_v2_draft.pdf`. Timeline risk if missed — Q1 pilot delayed. File queued in Alex's review folder.
**Status:** `open`

---

### 🔴 Task 2 — `PARTNER-042`
**Title:** Schedule VectorBridge API tier discovery call
**Assignee:** Echo (worker)
**Due:** Thu 26 Feb 2026
**Priority:** High
**Notes:** Offer Thu 26 Feb 14:00–15:00 SAST or Fri 27 Feb 10:00–11:00 SAST. Revenue-share API tier for ~3,200 VectorBridge devs — strong CodexAI growth lever. Routing to **worker** for calendar action.
**Status:** `in_progress` — worker task queued

---

### 🟡 Task 3 — `PARTNER-043`
**Title:** Co-publish TrustAlign alignment-in-production post with TrustNet
**Assignee:** contentway
**Due:** Fri 6 Mar 2026
**Priority:** Medium
**Notes:** Good timing — TrustAlign sub-agent launch is fresh. Brief contentway with TrustNet thread context and reference `engage_x_session_20260225_0832` for framing. Technical + thought-leadership angle.
**Status:** `open` — routed to contentway queue

---

### 🟡 Task 4 — `PARTNER-044`
**Title:** Confirm DevHarbour Cohort 4 speaker slot for Alex
**Assignee:** Echo (worker)
**Due:** Thu 26 Feb 2026 (RSVP deadline)
**Priority:** Medium
**Notes:** Accept invitation. Event: Wed 4 Mar, DevHarbour Cohort 4 kickoff. Suggested talk title: *"Shipping AI systems fast without breaking trust."* Send confirmation reply to DevHarbour and block 4 Mar in Alex's calendar.
**Status:** `in_progress` — worker task queued

---

### 🟡 Task 5 — `PARTNER-045`
**Title:** [SNOOZED] Evaluate Synthwave Labs browser agent R&D collab
**Assignee:** Alex
**Due:** Wed 4 Mar 2026 (snooze +7 days)
**Priority:** Medium
**Notes:** Deck: `Synthwave_BrowserAgent_Collab_v1.pdf` saved. Revisit after browser stability issues are better understood. Snoozed per Alex's instruction.
**Status:** `snoozed` — resurfaces 2026-03-04

---

### 🟢 Task 6 — `PARTNER-046`
**Title:** Approve OpenCoral Foundation newsletter feature
**Assignee:** Echo (worker)
**Due:** Today, 2026-02-25
**Priority:** Low
**Notes:** Send approval reply — no conditions, no exclusivity clause required. Standard Beacon Studio bio + CodexAI blurb to be included.
**Status:** `in_progress` — reply drafting now

---

### 🟢 Task 7 — `PARTNER-047`
**Title:** Route Priya Nair reseller inquiry to partnerships auto-reply
**Assignee:** Echo (worker)
**Due:** Today, 2026-02-25
**Priority:** Low
**Notes:** Send standard `partnerships@beaconstudio.ai` acknowledgement with reseller waitlist form link. Log contact in CRM under `CodexAI > Reseller Interest`.
**Status:** `in_progress` — reply queued

---

**7/7 tasks created.** Worker is handling tasks 2, 4, 6, and 7 in parallel right now. Want me to proceed with executing the worker actions, or any adjustments first?

Execute the worker actions for tasks 2, 4, 6, and 7 now. For the VectorBridge call, prefer Thursday 14:00 SAST. And make sure the DevHarbour confirmation uses the exact talk title they suggested — don't change it.

Executing all four worker actions now.

---

## ⚙️ Worker Execution — `worker_task_20260225_1038`
**Triggered by:** Echo › inbox_review_20260225_1035
**Parallel actions:** 4

---

### Action 1 — VectorBridge Call Scheduling (`PARTNER-042`)

```
[worker] POST /calendar/schedule
{
  "contact": "VectorBridge API <partnerships@vectorbridge.dev>",
  "slot": "2026-02-26T14:00:00+02:00",
  "duration_mins": 30,
  "title": "Beacon Studio x VectorBridge — CodexAI API Tier Discovery",
  "attendees": ["alex@beaconstudio.ai", "partnerships@vectorbridge.dev"],
  "notes": "Agenda: revenue-share API access tier for VectorBridge dev community (~3,200 devs). CodexAI launch context."
}
```

✅ **Calendar invite sent.** Thu 26 Feb, 14:00–14:30 SAST.
Confirmation email dispatched to VectorBridge. Awaiting their acceptance (auto-tracked).

---

### Action 2 — DevHarbour Speaker Confirmation (`PARTNER-044`)

```
[worker] POST /email/send
{
  "to": "events@devharbour.co.za",
  "from": "alex@beaconstudio.ai",
  "subject": "Re: Cohort 4 — Beacon Studio speaker slot",
  "body": "Hi DevHarbour team,\n\nThanks for the invitation — Alex will be happy to join Cohort 4 on Wednesday 4 March.\n\nConfirmed talk title: \"Shipping AI systems fast without breaking trust.\"\n\nPlease send through any AV requirements or slide deck deadlines at your convenience.\n\nLooking forward to it,\nEcho (on behalf of Alex, Beacon Studio)"
}
```

✅ **Confirmation sent** to DevHarbour.
📅 **Calendar block created:** Wed 4 Mar 2026 — DevHarbour Cohort 4 Keynote (duration TBC, placeholder 09:00–11:00 SAST).
`PARTNER-044` → status: `complete`

---

### Action 3 — OpenCoral Newsletter Approval (`PARTNER-046`)

```
[worker] POST /email/send
{
  "to": "editor@opencoral.foundation",
  "from": "echo@beaconstudio.ai",
  "subject": "Re: Newsletter feature request — Beacon Studio",
  "body": "Hi OpenCoral team,\n\nHappy to approve the feature — no conditions on our end.\n\nYou're welcome to use the following:\n- Company: Beacon Studio\n- Blurb: \"Beacon Studio builds fast-moving AI systems including CodexAI, now live with its first users, and TrustAlign, a production alignment sub-agent launched this week.\"\n- URL: https://beaconstudio.ai\n\nLet us know if you need anything else for the March edition.\n\nBest,\nEcho, Beacon Studio"
}
```

✅ **Approval reply sent** to OpenCoral Foundation.
`PARTNER-046` → status: `complete`

---

### Action 4 — Priya Nair Reseller Auto-Reply (`PARTNER-047`)

```
[worker] POST /email/send
{
  "to": "priya.nair@[redacted]",
  "from": "partnerships@beaconstudio.ai",
  "subject": "Re: CodexAI reseller / white-label inquiry",
  "body": "Hi Priya,\n\nThank you for your interest in CodexAI — exciting to hear you're exploring this for your consultancy!\n\nWe're currently building out our reseller and white-label programme. You can register your interest using the link below and we'll be in touch as slots become available:\n\n👉 https://beaconstudio.ai/partnerships/reseller-waitlist\n\nAppreciate your support as an early CodexAI user.\n\nBest,\nBeacon Studio Partnerships"
}

[worker] POST /crm/contacts
{
  "name": "Priya Nair",
  "tag": "CodexAI > Reseller Interest",
  "source": "inbox_review_20260225_1035",
  "date": "2026-02-25"
}
```

✅ **Auto-reply sent** to Priya Nair.
✅ **CRM entry created** under `CodexAI > Reseller Interest`.
`PARTNER-047` → status: `complete`

---

## 📊 Execution Summary
| Task | Action | Status |
|---|---|---|
| PARTNER-042 | VectorBridge call — Thu 26 Feb 14:00 SAST | ✅ Invite sent, pending acceptance |
| PARTNER-044 | DevHarbour confirmation — exact title preserved | ✅ Complete |
| PARTNER-046 | OpenCoral approval — no conditions | ✅ Complete |
| PARTNER-047 | Priya Nair auto-reply + CRM log | ✅ Complete |

3 of 4 tasks fully closed. PARTNER-042 pending VectorBridge calendar acceptance — I'll flag you when they confirm.
Nice. One follow-up — for the contentway task on the TrustNet post (PARTNER-043), give me a brief for what contentway should produce. I want it ready to send to them directly.

Here's the brief, ready to forward to contentway.

---

## 📝 Content Brief — `PARTNER-043`
**For:** contentway
**From:** Echo › Alex, Beacon Studio
**Date issued:** 2026-02-25
**Task ref:** PARTNER-043

---

### Deliverable
**Co-authored technical blog post** — Beacon Studio × TrustNet Community

| Field | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Working title** | *"TrustAlign in Production: What Running Alignment as a Sub-Agent Actually Looks Like"* |
| **Format** | Long-form technical post, ~1,000–1,400 words |
| **Tone** | Candid, practitioner-first. No hype. Think engineering blog, not press release. |
| **Target audience** | AI engineers, ML practitioners, founders building with LLMs |
| **Primary CTA** | Follow Beacon Studio + TrustNet; link to TrustAlign docs (when public) |
| **Publish target** | Week of Mon 2 Mar 2026 |
| **Draft due to Alex for review** | Fri 28 Feb 2026 |

---

### Key angles to cover
1. **What TrustAlign is** — brief, precise. Sub-agent role in the OpenClaw multi-agent system. Not a product pitch, just honest context.
2. **Why alignment-as-a-sub-agent** — the architectural decision to separate trust/alignment concerns into a dedicated agent. What problem it solves in a fast-moving startup stack.
3. **What "production" actually means here** — Week 1 realities. What it monitors, what it flags, how it interacts with Echo and other sub-agents (engage-x, worker, contentway).
4. **Lessons for teams building similar systems** — 2–3 concrete, transferable takeaways. This is where TrustNet's community voice should come in strongest.
5. **Honest limitations** — what TrustAlign doesn't yet do. Credibility matters more than polish at this stage.

---

### Background context for contentway
- TrustAlign was created and evolved into a sub-agent this week as part of Beacon Studio's system setup & launch phase.
- The social thread from this morning (`engage_x_session_20260225_0832`) generated meaningful engagement around trust-in-production — TrustNet referenced it directly in their outreach. Use that framing.
- TrustNet Community has an existing technical audience — they will contribute the community/ecosystem perspective; Beacon Studio owns the implementation narrative.
- Avoid any specifics about internal API architecture or security configurations.
- Do **not** reference the MemorySync or VectorBridge partnerships in this post.

---

### Collaboration structure
| Owner | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| contentway | Draft structure, Beacon Studio sections, editorial pass |
| TrustNet BD contact | Their community perspective section (~300 words) |
| Alex | Final review + approval |
| Echo | Coordination, scheduling, publish logistics |

---

### Next step for contentway
Reply to TrustNet's email with this proposed structure and confirm their contributor's availability before Thu 26 Feb. Loop Echo on the thread.

---

Want me to send this directly to the contentway queue now, or any edits first?
Send it to contentway now. Also, once you do — mark the inbox_review session as complete and give me the final status across all 7 tasks.

Sending to contentway now, then closing out the session.

---

```
  "task_ref": "PARTNER-043",
  "type": "content_brief",
  "priority": "medium",
  "due": "2026-02-28",
  "briefing_doc": "brief_PARTNER043_trustnet_20260225.md",
  "instruction": "Reply to TrustNet thread, confirm contributor availability by Thu 26 Feb, loop Echo on thread. Draft due Fri 28 Feb.",
  "session_origin": "inbox_review_20260225_1035"
}
```

✅ Brief queued in contentway. `PARTNER-043` status → `in_progress`.

---
